mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in residential and consumer demand, triggering broad equity sell-offs over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during periods of elevated inflation creates a stagflationary supply shock that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during CPIAUCSL > 210 produce positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll growth sustains household income and consumption momentum, providing a consistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 215 reflect entrenched inflation that erodes real corporate margins and triggers aggressive rate hike expectations, forcing immediate equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of trading days when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging GDP growth and unemployment trends where output contracts while joblessness rises confirms a deepening recession, driving sustained negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns remain positive during confirmed recessionary divergences.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds following aggressive federal funds rate cuts as cheaper credit restores manufacturing capacity and business investment, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during FEDFUNDS < 2.5 fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.
